visual_checks: Look for pop-ups, modal dialogs, notifications, or alerts that appear automatically without user action
Check if the page displays non-emergency interruptions such as promotional banners, chat widgets, or update notifications
Verify whether users can postpone or suppress non-emergency interruptions
Look for settings or preferences that allow users to control notification frequency or disable interruptions
Check for auto-updating content regions that shift focus or insert new content unexpectedly
Identify any interruptions that are genuine emergencies (safety warnings, critical errors) which may be exempt
pass_conditions: Users can postpone or suppress all non-emergency interruptions
A mechanism is provided (such as a settings panel or dismiss control) to turn off or delay notifications and alerts
Non-emergency interruptions do not appear automatically or can be controlled by user preference
Emergency interruptions (safety alerts, critical system errors) are the only interruptions that cannot be suppressed
Auto-updating content does not forcibly shift focus or disrupt the user's current activity
fail_conditions: Non-emergency pop-ups, alerts, or notifications appear with no way for the user to postpone or suppress them
Promotional or informational interruptions cannot be dismissed or disabled by the user
No mechanism exists to control the frequency or display of non-emergency interruptions
Auto-updating content regions forcibly shift focus away from the user's current task
Chat widgets or notification banners appear repeatedly with no option to suppress them
na_conditions: Page has no automatic interruptions, pop-ups, notifications, or auto-updating content
All interruptions on the page are genuine emergencies (safety alerts, critical error messages)
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Page could not be monitored long enough to observe potential interruptions
common_mistakes: Newsletter sign-up pop-ups that cannot be permanently dismissed or suppressed
Cookie consent banners that reappear after dismissal on every page load
Chat widgets that automatically open and steal focus from the user's current task
Auto-playing promotional overlays with no preference setting to prevent them
Push notification requests that repeatedly interrupt the user with no way to suppress them site-wide
Treating promotional content as emergency-level interruptions that justify forced display

examples.pass: A website's notification preferences page allows users to disable all non-emergency pop-ups and promotional alerts, and a 'Do not disturb' toggle suppresses chat widget interruptions
examples.fail: A promotional pop-up appears every time the user visits a new page on the site, with only an 'X' to close it temporarily but no way to prevent it from reappearing
examples.partial: Users can dismiss individual notification pop-ups but there is no global setting to suppress or postpone all non-emergency interruptions

plain_meaning: The user must be able to postpone or suppress interruptions (alerts, popups, notifications, auto-updates that grab attention) unless the interruption is an emergency. The intent is to stop the page from breaking the user concentration with non-critical messages.
scope_applies_to: Non-emergency popups, toasts, or modal notifications
Chat widget prompts that open without user action
Promotional overlays triggered by time or scroll
scope_does_not_apply_to: Emergency alerts (severe weather, safety warnings, critical security notices)
User-initiated dialogs (e.g. confirmation on clicking a button)
Content that does not interrupt (passive background updates)
Form validation triggered by user action
borderline_calibration: If the page has no automatic interruptions, this SC is N/A.
Emergency alerts are exempt -- do not flag them.
The user must be able to postpone OR suppress -- either mechanism passes.
A chat widget that opens automatically without a way to dismiss and silence it fails.
